Seattle, WA 98109
current_employment.reason_for_leaving:
I am looking to move into a role with a broader scope of responsibility and more direct involvement in service improvement and staff development. In my current position, I have progressed from Medical Office Administrator to Senior Medical Office Administrator, and over the past two years I have taken on many duties that overlap with practice management, including coordinating a small team of front-desk staff, helping to implement a new electronic health record system, and leading quality-improvement projects around patient scheduling and check-in workflows.

While I value my current employer and have a good relationship with my manager and colleagues, the organizational structure is fairly flat and there is limited room for further progression without waiting several years for a vacancy. I am particularly interested in roles where I can formally supervise staff, contribute to policy and procedure development, and be more involved in cross-department collaboration. I also live in Shoreline and am hoping to reduce my commute slightly by working in a role that is either hybrid or located a bit closer to home.

In short, I am not leaving due to any conflict or dissatisfaction with Evergreen Community Health Center, but rather because I feel ready for the next step in my career and want to apply the skills I have built in a setting where there is a clearer pathway for advancement and professional development.
current_employment.notice_you_need_to_give: Four weeks (one calendar month) notice as stated in my employment contract
current_employment.summary_of_current_job_description:
In my current role as Senior Medical Office Administrator at Evergreen Community Health Center, I am responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operations of the front office for a busy primary care and behavioral health clinic that serves approximately 120–150 patients per day.

Key responsibilities include:

• Front desk operations and patient flow: I supervise and support a team of three full-time and two part-time front-desk receptionists. This includes organizing daily coverage schedules, ensuring we have appropriate staffing during peak hours, and stepping in to cover the desk when needed. I monitor patient flow from check-in to check-out, troubleshoot bottlenecks (for example, when multiple providers are running behind), and communicate delays to patients in a clear and respectful manner.

• Patient registration and records management: I oversee new patient registrations, verify demographic and insurance information at each visit, and ensure that consent forms and privacy notices are up to date. I routinely audit patient records for accuracy (e.g., correct spelling of names, date of birth, and insurance policy numbers) and work with our medical records coordinator to resolve discrepancies or incomplete documentation. I am a designated super-user for our Epic-based electronic health record (EHR) system and provide informal one-on-one training for new staff on registration and scheduling workflows.

• Scheduling and coordination: I manage and optimize the appointment schedules for eight primary care providers, three behavioral health clinicians, and two nurse practitioners. This involves building and adjusting templates in the EHR, balancing urgent same-day appointments with routine follow-ups, and coordinating double-booking only when clinically appropriate. I also coordinate specialty referrals within our network, arrange interpreter services for non-English-speaking patients (primarily Spanish and Vietnamese), and help manage telehealth appointment logistics.

• Billing support and insurance verification: While I do not perform full medical billing, I am responsible for front-end revenue cycle tasks such as verifying insurance eligibility prior to visits, collecting co-pays at check-in, and explaining basic coverage information to patients in plain language. I regularly liaise with the billing department to resolve front-desk billing errors, update fee schedules at the point of service, and follow up on rejected claims related to registration issues.

• Quality improvement and reporting: I lead small quality-improvement initiatives focused on front-office processes. For example, I recently coordinated a project to reduce new patient no-show rates by implementing reminder calls and text messages 48 hours before appointments, which resulted in a measurable drop in no-shows over a six-month period. I compile monthly reports on front desk metrics (average check-in time, call abandonment rates, and same-day appointment utilization) and present these findings at our monthly operations meeting.

• Staff training, support, and performance feedback: As the senior administrator, I help onboard and train new front-desk staff, including walking them through our EHR workflows, patient privacy requirements, and customer service standards. I provide ongoing coaching, especially around handling difficult or distressed patients, and I give input into annual performance evaluations for the team. I also maintain our front desk procedure manual and update it when processes change.

• Customer service and problem resolution: I am a primary point of escalation for patient concerns related to scheduling, wait times, and general front-office issues. I frequently mediate between patients and clinical staff to clarify misunderstandings, reschedule appointments, or arrange follow-up calls from providers. I strive to de-escalate tense situations calmly and professionally, while still enforcing clinic policies and maintaining fairness to all patients.

Overall, my role requires strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to balance competing priorities in a fast-paced healthcare environment. I work closely with clinicians, nurses, behavioral health staff, and the billing and IT departments to ensure that patients have a smooth, respectful, and efficient experience from the moment they arrive to the time they leave the clinic.

how_you_heard_about_the_vacancy.how_did_you_become_aware_of_this_vacancy:
I first became aware of this vacancy through a job alert email from Indeed, which I receive weekly based on saved searches related to "clinic administrator," "practice manager," and "healthcare operations" in the greater Seattle area. I clicked through to the posting because the job title and description aligned closely with the responsibilities I have been gradually taking on in my current role, particularly around supervising administrative staff and contributing to operational improvements.

After seeing the alert, I went directly to your organization’s official website to read the full job description and to learn more about your services, patient population, and organizational values. I also looked up recent news articles and reviews to get a sense of your reputation in the community. The combination of the job responsibilities, the emphasis on patient-centered care, and the opportunities for professional growth convinced me that this was a strong fit, so I decided to apply.
how_you_heard_about_the_vacancy.media: Indeed.com (job search website) and your organization’s official careers webpage
